what_changed: No defined basic operation existed at the start; by the end, one primitive operator was identified and confirmed as the simplest possible starting point.
Two concepts that were treated as separate were shown to produce each other in a reciprocal loop.
A property measured in one configuration was shown to remain stable when tested across multiple different configurations.
A hypothesized cause of a correlation was tested by intervening on it and shown not to produce the correlation.
A second hypothesized cause of a correlation was compared against the correlation and shown not to produce it.
A measured property was found to hold up in relative ranking across different measurement methods, but its absolute value shifted depending on which method was used.
Among seven metrics compared pairwise, one was found to be a mathematical projection of another, and most pairwise relationships turned out to be redundant.
An observed phenomenon was tested against measurement parameters and found to be a binary artifact of how the data was discretized, not a real feature.
The underlying structure of a system was shown to be more fundamental than the specific collection of elements comprising it.
The process of preserving distinctions was shown to be more fundamental than the distinctions themselves.
What counted as a pattern was shown to depend on both the world being described and the representation used to describe it, rather than being an objective feature of the world alone.
A method of explanation assumed to be reliable was shown to fail 60 percent of the time across tested cases.
The order in which operations were performed was shown not to affect the final result, confirmed across eleven different orderings.
Four components that were initially treated as independent were shown to depend on each other in a sequential chain.
An apparent progression across multiple examinations was shown to be an artifact of the examination ordering rather than a genuine shift.
What appeared to be linear forward progress was shown to be a cyclical pattern that repeated rather than converged.
A mechanism whose role was unclear was found to be present in every observed case of improvement, making it a necessary condition.
Four elements previously studied in isolation were shown to require a specific combination of all four together to produce a sustained effect; removing any one broke the configuration.
Objects were reconceived from static things with fixed properties to accumulated traces left by past processes.
A claim based on initial analysis was re-examined but did not gain additional support from the re-examination.
